title: Read the disassembly to verify behavior
description: Confirm what the machine actually does by reading the disassembly and stepping it, rather than trusting the source or mnemonics alone.
body: Use when validating low-level behavior or debugging an assembly/codegen issue. Do not assume the source assembled to what you intended or that a mnemonic means what you think on this operand width. Actions: disassemble the function (objdump / debugger / Godbolt), confirm register usage, operand sizes, and addressing modes, and single-step in a debugger watching registers, flags, and the stack at each instruction. Validation signals include the stepped register/stack state matching expectation and the result matching a reference. Risk boundary: assembly bugs manifest as corrupted state far from the instruction at fault; reading and stepping the disassembly is how you localize them — guessing from source is unreliable.
